info.title: CPay Bank Service API
info.description: API documentation for the CPay Bank Service, an electronic wallet and banking application built using Go, chi, and MongoDB.

get.description: A simple health check or welcome endpoint returning a plain text message.
Redirects the client to the specified OAuth provider's authorization page (e.g. github, google).

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: The name of the OAuth provider (e.g. google, github)

307.description: Temporary Redirect to the OAuth provider login page.

get.description: Callback endpoint invoked by the OAuth provider. Processes the state/code and logs the user in, setting the refresh token in HTTP-only cookies. Redirects the client back to the frontend.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: The name of the OAuth provider

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: State parameter passed back by OAuth provider to protect against CSRF.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Authorization code exchanged for access token.

200.description: Success response when simulated/injected user is active.

307.description: Temporary Redirect to frontend (home page or /auth/login/incomplate for incomplete registers).

post.description: Completes registration for a user whose status is 'UNCOMPLATE' by creating their first wallet and PIN.

get.description: Refreshes and returns a new Access Token. It reads the Refresh Token from cookies and sets a new Access Token in the cookies.
Logs out the current user, invalidates their session, and deletes auth cookies.
Retrieves the profile information of the currently authenticated user.
Retrieves all wallets owned by the currently authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a new wallet for the currently authenticated user.

put.description: Sets one of the user's wallets as the primary wallet.

get.description: Fetches wallet details using its 12-digit account number.

parameters.name: account_number
parameters.description: The 12-digit account number of the wallet

post.description: Submits a request to top up a wallet. Requires approval from an administrator to complete.
Transfers funds from the current user's wallet to a destination wallet using its account number.

400.description: Bad request (insufficient balance, incorrect PIN, invalid destination wallet).

get.description: Lists all payment codes generated by the current user.

post.description: Pays an active payment code created by another user.

400.description: Invalid PIN, code expired/cancelled, or insufficient funds.

post.description: Generates a unique 14-digit payment code (starting with 'PY') that other users can pay.

get.description: Retrieves the public details of a payment code using the 14-digit string.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The 14-digit payment code string starting with PY

delete.description: Allows the creator of a payment code to cancel it.

403.description: Forbidden (not the owner of the code).

get.description: Retrieves the transaction history of the current user.
Retrieves detailed information of a transaction using its reference code.
Retrieves the transaction history associated with a specific wallet account number.

parameters.name: account_number
parameters.description: The 12-digit account number of the wallet

get.description: Admin only endpoint to retrieve all registered users (paginated).
Admin only endpoint to retrieve details of a specific user. (Cannot retrieve self details here).

404.description: User not found or requesting own profile.

get.description: Admin only endpoint to retrieve all wallets of a specific user.

put.description: Admin only endpoint to suspend a user's account/wallets.
Admin only endpoint to activate a suspended user account/wallets.

get.description: Admin only endpoint to list all top-up requests.
Admin only endpoint to get details of a specific top-up request.

put.description: Admin only endpoint to approve a pending top-up request. This adds balance to the user's wallet.
Admin only endpoint to reject a pending top-up request.

get.description: Admin only endpoint to view all generated payment codes.
Admin only endpoint to get details of a specific payment code.
Admin only endpoint to view payment codes generated by a specific user.

delete.description: Admin only endpoint to cancel a payment code.

role.description: User roles represented as flags: 1 = RoleUser (1<<0), 2 = RoleAdmin (1<<1).
